description: Open-source Python library that connects any LLM to any MCP server. Enables agentic workflows by bridging LLM providers with the Model Context Protocol.

network_scope.description: Connects to any MCP server endpoint specified by the host application. Outbound only; scope depends on which MCP servers are configured.
outbound_targets: configurable by host — any MCP server URL

trust_rationale: Popular open-source library (10k+ stars) maintained by Manufact. Source is public and MIT-licensed. No creator claim on OpenTrust registry yet; trust level reflects community reputation, not registry verification.

security_flags.title: Arbitrary MCP server execution
security_flags.detail: mcp-use spawns MCP server processes as specified by the host configuration. A malicious or misconfigured server config could cause mcp-use to execute untrusted code. Host applications must validate server configurations before passing to mcp-use.

security_flags.title: LLM prompt injection via tool responses
security_flags.detail: Tool call results from MCP servers are forwarded to the LLM without sanitization. Malicious tool output could attempt prompt injection. Applications should audit which MCP servers they connect mcp-use to.

cost_model.notes: mcp-use itself is free and open-source. LLM API costs are borne by the host application.
